videos.title: Cross Country Skiing   Ramsau 2020
videos.description:
Cross country skiing is an engaging and attractive sport.  So what is involved?  
If you want to learn how to make videos like this see  https://www.cambridgetvschool.co.uk/courses/
The London Nordic skiing Club led a week long trip to Ramsau, Austria, in January 2020 and this video describes the sport and the experiences of members. 

Filmed and edited by Bob Coates, Cambridge TV

Music: ES_If You Really Believe (Instrumental Version) - Tomas Skyldeberg

With many thanks to Dr Stella Thompson, Pauline Styles, & Chris Jones for their contributions. And also to Peter Moysey & Dagmar Junghanns for putting up with a camera following their every move...
videos.transcript:
damaged for travelling in a play
[Music]
one inch
- thank you - the race start
the activity itself is really enjoyable
getting out on the snow moving along
comfortably feeling good about moving on
the skis and then just getting up into
beautiful remote places where it's very
peaceful and very tranquil and meeting
some great people along the way this
week actually encompasses quite a lot of
what we want to do on snow enable people
to improve their technique with training
recreational skiing and Racing's my
biggest ambition is to get good enough
technically to be able to carry on
skiing for another 10 or 15 years that
are important to have in your repertoire
art to be able to ski reasonably
efficiently and fast descend safely
without endangering yourself or other
people navigate safely off track be able
to cope in thick snow and get up when
you fall over those things are actually
surprisingly difficult and survive in a
cold environment there is one
fundamental truth about all
cross-country skiing and that is you
have to balance on one foot the movement
that you do is all about trying to get
you gliding and and that glide is always
on on the one foot at a time and then
you move again and you're gliding on the
other foot so just put your hand on your
hip I'm taking your arm back over your
head because if you look down you'll you
tend to sort of go for lower and lower
the bar at the front of the boots that
bar goes in that groove there and then
you push push the whole of the ball of
your foot down the health benefits are
enormous it's not just that it's it's a
really good all-around exercise that
pushes your heart rate up and makes you
fitter and stronger it also encouraged
you encourages you to maintain a healthy
weight because it's a bit harder if
you're overweight it's good for the mind
in the sense that you get out into
lovely places where you can feel
peaceful and calm so if you're anxious
or stressed about things
but that can help and it's just very
good for self-confidence and building
self-confidence I would encourage
everyone to have a go at cross-country
skiing even if you intend to primarily
be an alpine skier and the simple reason
is back to the central point of
cross-country skiing which is it
improves your balance so it's much much
safer than downhill you don't have to
wear a helmet you're often you're on
much softer snow you're not going as
fast you're not going down the same kind
of steep descents is usually time and
space to avoid other people you also not
like you to come back with a head injury
or a broken limb there are two types of
cross-country skiing two main types the
classic style and the skating style and
the most important thing I would say is
that they were very very complimentary
classic is much more like hiking on skis
in that you're you're moving one leg in
front of another and using your arms to
pole as you go along so anybody who's
ever done Nordic Walking will have a
rough idea of how it might work
everything moves nicely straight
backwards and forwards and you use the
grooves that are in the snow and it's
it's a very relaxed style when when you
manage to master it a little bit skating
on skis is much more like ice skating so
if you've ever ice skated you'll know
that you have to try and push your legs
out to gain propulsion and that I find
personally much harder but it is a
completely different method of
propelling yourself on skis skating is
faster in in the equivalent conditions
if you're particularly if you're flat
and traveling long distance the
equipment is different it's really
elegant it looks lovely and it's fun to
do it depends I think what your
background is how easy you would find it
to do most people would start with
classic and then how to go at skate once
they're a bit more confident on skis
going uphill in skate skiing is probably
the most awkward looking movement in in
skate skiing and and you do have to be a
little bit more agile
than than any of the other movements
because you have to do get your feet
quite wide apart and to me it always
looks like I sort of frog trying to
sidestep up the hill the club currently
is trying to recruit some younger people
into the club that's the time to learn
we're seeing generally a lot of
youngsters that seem to be a lot more
interested in cross-country skiing we've
had some success nationally in getting
people into Winter Olympics and things
like that but if you look at the any of
their competitions the people that are
active in cross-country skiing are
magnificent sportsmen and in Europe the
crowds are enormous a lot of endurance
sports their hard work but they're fun
and you get a tremendous buzz and sense
of achievement for making it round a
circuit or doing it well or getting up
onto the top of a hill and seeing the
view but even if you've never skied it's
not too late to start and you can still
learn to do it and you will have a lot
of fun and enjoy it very much

videos.title: Fischer SKILETICS® Park in Ramsau am Dachstein skating
2023 Para Nordic Skiing World Champ. - Cross-country skiing: Best Images of Day 9 | Paralympic Games
videos.description:
The 2023 FIS Para Nordic Skiing World Championships took place from 20 to 29 January in Östersund, Sweden. Prepare to be delighted with the best images of Para cross-country skiing  - Day 9.

The International Paralympic Committee's vision is to make for an inclusive world through Para sport. Our mission is to lead the Paralympic Movement, oversee the delivery of the Paralympic Games and support members to enable Para athletes to achieve sporting excellence.

SUBSCRIBE for more great Paralympic content ▶ https://www.youtube.com/c/paralympics

More information about the Paralympics can be found on the Paralympic website ▶ https://www.paralympic.org/

videos.title: H.S. Nordic skiing championships held at Rikert
videos.description:
Middlebury sweeps boys, girls D2 crowns
  
 For more Local News from WCAX:  https://www.wcax.com/
 For more YouTube Content: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCn7285bO_vEziJ9brPtkFMA
videos.transcript:
High School Nordic skiing championships
got underway with freestyle races last
week in craftsbury and on Tuesday they
wrapped things up with the classic races
from the Reichert Nordic Center starting
in D1 boys it was Mount Anthony's Luke
rizio who was the star of the day
carrying over his momentum from last
week he won the individual Race by 40
seconds then how about almost a full
minute on Tuesday Mount Anthony claiming
the top three spots in that morning race
rizio followed by Peter McKenna and
Finnegan Payne right behind him that
would set up a dominant relay in the
afternoon as well the Patriots heavy
favorites and they lived up to it look
at the gap between them in second place
after just the first lap they'd go on to
win it going away and take the team
title in second Mount Mansfield a cougar
team with a healthy gap between them in
third place which at the time was Saint
Jay tough conditions though causing a
slip near the final turn in the
background there that would open the
door for cvu to charge into the final
Podium spot the RedHawks finishing in
third over in D2 Middlebury with a
strong showing in the morning session
the Tigers claiming the top two
individuals spots courtesy of Elliot
Schneider and Baxter Harrington the
relay though it was tight in the middle
of the pack especially early on some
close finishes but Middlebury staying
hot the Tigers cleaning the top spot in
that relay and overall for the D2 boys
filed by Montpelier in second in
Woodstock in third on to the girls race
starting in D1 it was once again a good
day to be a seahorse Burlington claiming
the top three spots in this morning's
classic race that was led by Jillian
Fairfax followed by Greta Kilburn and
Elsa Sanborn that would position them
nicely for the Relay as well a steady
four times by the seahorses capped off
by Fairfax tagging Kilburn giving VHS
plenty to be proud of in the top spot in
the D1 relay and overall for the third
consecutive season the seahorses
followed by Mount Anthony in second and
cvu in third in division two Middlebury
would Place three racers in the top five
in the classic race but it was u-32's
Claire Serrano who finished with the top
individual time however the Tigers
validate their individual performances
in the morning during that relay check
out the gap between Mary Harrington she
tags Ava Schneider here for the final
leg the Tigers all smiles Schneider
crosses the Finish Line a dominant first
place team finish they sweep the boys
and girls D2 title second place going to
montpelier's girls with Margaret voice
and anchoring the Finish third place
captured by the Raiders of u32
